original_hypothesis.title: Referral Fee Calculated But Never Transferred to Referrer
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In _handleTransfers, referralFeeCost is computed and emitted in the Purchased event, but no actual token transfer to the referral address occurs. The entire buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal is sent to feeCollector, keeping the referral's share. The referral system is completely non-functional despite events suggesting otherwise.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a clear code bug: the referral amount is calculated, a variable is set, it's emitted in events, but the transfer is missing. FeeCollector receives funds that should go to referrers. Any integrator building referral tracking will have incorrect accounting.

proof_of_signal.pattern: referralFeeCost computed at line 295-298, but feeCollectorTotal at line 300 = buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal (does not subtract referralFeeCost). No safeTransfer to _referral address exists.

preconditions: A purchase is made with a non-zero _referral address

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: referralFeeCost = buyerFeeTotal - (baseAmount * bfee * referralFee) / (BASE * BASE) — computed but never used in transfer
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: feeCollector silently receives referral portion. No transfer(referral, referralFeeCost) exists in code.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: FeeCollector gains 100% of referral rewards across all referred purchases. With referralFee=1000, ~90% of buyer fee should go to referrer but goes to feeCollector instead.

economic_questions: What total referral fees would be misdirected over expected marketplace volume?
Is the referral formula itself also inverted (90% vs 10%)?
Are there downstream integrators relying on Purchased event's referralReward field?
validation_requirements: Confirm no transfer to _referral exists anywhere in the purchase flow
Verify feeCollectorTotal does not deduct referralFeeCost
Test with actual purchase and verify referrer balance unchanged

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: N/A — protocol design flaw, not attacker exploit
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: FeeCollector receives all referral fees — potentially significant protocol revenue leakage

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Referral program is non-functional, undermining user acquisition incentives

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All referrers who promote the marketplace expecting rewards
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All referral fees across marketplace lifetime

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace exact token flows in _handleTransfers with referral set

validation_result.impact_summary: Referral fees are computed and emitted in events but never transferred to referrers. The feeCollector silently receives 100% of all referral rewards, rendering the referral system completely non-functional.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The _handleTransfers function in SecondSwap_Marketplace.sol computes referralFeeCost when a valid referral address is provided and the listing is not whitelisted. However, the function never executes a token transfer to the _referral address. The feeCollectorTotal is calculated as buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal without subtracting referralFeeCost. The entire fee amount goes to feeCollector. The referralFeeCost value is only returned from the function and subsequently emitted in the Purchased event, misleading off-chain integrators into believing referral payments occurred.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal is accurate. In _handleTransfers, referralFeeCost is computed but: (1) feeCollectorTotal on the line `uint256 feeCollectorTotal = (buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal)` does not subtract referralFeeCost, and (2) there is no `safeTransfer` call to the `_referral` address anywhere in the function or in spotPurchase. The only three transfer operations are: safeTransferFrom(buyer -> contract), safeTransfer(contract -> seller), and safeTransfer(contract -> feeCollector). The referral address is never a recipient of any transfer.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: referralFeeCost is computed but never transferred to _referral. feeCollectorTotal = buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal does not subtract referralFeeCost. No safeTransfer to _referral exists. The feeCollector receives the full fee amount including the portion that should go to the referrer.

calling_sequence: 6. Inside _handleTransfers: baseAmount computed, buyerFeeTotal and sellerFeeTotal calculated
7. safeTransferFrom(buyer, contract, baseAmount + buyerFeeTotal) - buyer pays
8. referralFeeCost computed as buyerFeeTotal - (baseAmount * bfee * referralFee)/(BASE*BASE)
10. feeCollectorTotal = buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal (NO subtraction of referralFeeCost)
11. safeTransfer(feeCollector, feeCollectorTotal) - feeCollector gets ALL fees including referral portion
13. referralFeeCost returned and emitted in Purchased event, misleading observers
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: In _handleTransfers, after computing referralFeeCost, the code proceeds to transfer the entire buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal to the feeCollector. There is no code path that transfers any tokens to the _referral address. The referralFeeCost variable is only used as a return value, which is then emitted in the Purchased event. Token flow: buyer pays (baseAmount + buyerFeeTotal), seller receives (baseAmount - sellerFeeTotal), feeCollector receives (buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal). Total out = baseAmount + buyerFeeTotal = total in. The accounting balances but referrer gets zero.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: The MarketplaceSetting constructor sets referralFee = 1000 (10%), confirming the referral system is intended to be active. No deployment scripts modify this to zero.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attacker exploit but a protocol-level code defect. Any purchase with a non-zero _referral address and a non-whitelisted listing will compute referralFeeCost, emit it in the Purchased event as referralReward, but transfer zero tokens to the referrer. The feeCollector receives the full buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal. With default referralFee=1000, the referralFeeCost formula yields buyerFeeTotal * (1 - 1000/10000) = buyerFeeTotal * 0.9 = 90% of buyer fee should go to referrer (note: the formula itself may be inverted — giving 90% to referrer instead of 10% — but since nothing is transferred, the inversion is moot). Every referred purchase is affected.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. The referral incentive system is completely non-functional despite having full event infrastructure suggesting it works. Any referral tracking system built on the Purchased event's referralReward field will show incorrect data — it will report rewards being paid that were never actually transferred. The feeCollector accumulates all referral fees that should have been distributed. Over the lifetime of the marketplace, this represents 100% loss of all referral rewards.
recommended_mitigations: Add a safeTransfer to the _referral address for referralFeeCost when referralFeeCost > 0
Subtract referralFeeCost from feeCollectorTotal: uint256 feeCollectorTotal = (buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal - referralFeeCost)
Review the referralFeeCost formula semantics — currently gives ~90% of buyer fee to referrer when referralFee=1000 (10%), which may be inverted from intent
Consider adding a test that verifies referrer balance increases after a referred purchase
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy marketplace with default settings (referralFee=1000, buyerFee=250). 2. Create a listing with whitelist=address(0). 3. Record referrer's token balance. 4. Call spotPurchase with a non-zero _referral address. 5. Verify referrer's balance is unchanged (zero transfer). 6. Verify feeCollector received buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal (full amount, no deduction). 7. Check Purchased event emits non-zero referralReward despite no actual transfer.
uncertainties: Whether the referral formula semantics are also inverted (90% vs 10% to referrer) is a secondary issue since no transfer occurs regardless
additional_testing_needed: Integration test confirming referrer balance unchanged after referred purchase
Verify feeCollector receives exact buyerFeeTotal + sellerFeeTotal with no deduction
Test with various referralFee values (1000, 5000, 10000) to confirm no transfer in any case
